name: Loom API
what_it_does: Async video messaging platform API for managing, sharing, and embedding screen recordings and video messages within team workflows.
use_cases: Retrieving and organizing Loom video links from agent workflows
Embedding Loom recordings in generated reports or tickets
Searching a team's video library by title or creator
Programmatic access to video metadata and transcripts
Integrating video walkthroughs into onboarding automation
not_for: Video hosting for public-facing content (use Mux or Vimeo)
best_when: You need to integrate async video messaging into team tools and workflows, especially for internal communication or customer support.
avoid_when: You need general video hosting, encoding control, or public video distribution.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 only. No API key option for server-side automation. User authorization required for accessing video libraries.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: limited videos, 5-min video cap, basic features
pricing.notes: API access available on paid plans. Free tier suitable only for individual testing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Limited API documentation. Error messages are basic HTTP codes with minimal context.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth-only auth makes server-side automation cumbersome - no API key for background agents
API is limited in scope - primarily read access, cannot create recordings programmatically
Rate limits are undocumented - agents may hit limits without clear guidance
API is relatively new and has limited endpoint coverage compared to the web app
Loom acquired by Atlassian in 2023 - API direction and availability may change
